page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is a virtual machine instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU consists of 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 504 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for integer and tile operations, and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. The instance includes 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage distributed across 5 local SSDs, but does not feature any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core and text-processing capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core, single-threaded gzip compression, single-threaded bzip3 decompression, and image processing. Multi-core performance is strong in Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. In database and memory operations, the server demonstrates average performance in Passmark database operations and memory bandwidth tests. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput both rank in the strong tier. For LLM inference, text generation with small models ranks in the top tier, while prompt processing for medium and large models ranks in the strong tier.

The c4-standard-32-lssd provides a balanced resource density with high-speed local NVMe storage, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring fast local scratch space or high-throughput database operations without the overhead of network-attached storage. However, the 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU represents a potential tradeoff for memory-intensive applications that might require a higher memory-to-core ratio. This server is suited for high-performance web serving, database hosting, text and image processing pipelines, and localized machine learning inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 16 physical cores. It includes 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 5 drives, with no GPU accelerators. The CPU supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions, delivering top-tier performance in single-core benchmarks, image processing, and single-threaded compression. Multi-core CPU performance, cryptographic operations, and static web serving throughput are strong, while memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. With 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU, this instance offers a balanced resource profile. It is suited for high-throughput database hosting, web serving, text processing, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd features 32 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and 1745 GB local NVMe SSD storage for database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, advanced matrix extensions, and strong single-core performance.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 120 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor
1745 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across 5 drives
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Top-tier single-core, image processing, and text processing performance
Strong throughput for Redis operations and static web serving
Suited for database hosting, web serving, and localized LLM inference
